(auto) ensure agent ends day at home

diary: Started the day the way I like it—quiet on the patio with coffee and my plants. The commute through Georgetown was smooth; I timed it right. Work was steady: case notes, check-ins with a few of the kids. One of them, Marcus, finally opened up about applying to community college. That felt good. Stayed inside at Analog for lunch because the heat was already climbing and that smoke from BC was making the air look hazy. Spent the afternoon catching up on paperwork, which isn't glamorous but necessary. By evening I was tired but restless, so after changing into something cooler at home, I decided to head to Gas Works for the concert. The park was packed—so many people trying to escape the heat. I found a quiet corner on the grass and just sat there, listening to the music drift across the water, feeling the breeze pick up. Sometimes you need that kind of simple thing to reset.
thought_process: Today felt like a balance between duty and permission to rest. I chose to avoid peak traffic in the morning because my mental bandwidth was already claimed by case work—I knew the afternoon would be heavy with follow-ups. Staying indoors during lunch made sense with the air quality and heat; I wasn't going to push myself. The concert decision came late, almost impulsively. Part of me wanted to collapse at home, but I recognized that restless energy and honored it. The heat and smoke created a kind of pressure in the city, and being around other people seeking the same escape—that concert crowd—felt oddly communal even though I sat alone. Work with youth keeps me grounded in purpose, but today I also needed to remember that I deserve small moments of ease.
world_event_prompt: Hot, clear Wednesday hitting 91F by mid-afternoon. Free outdoor concert at Gas Works Park from 6pm draws big crowds. Moderate air quality from distant BC wildfire smoke.
